formulas.name: Content
formulas.formula:
Here's a comprehensive formula sheet for CBSE Class 11 Economics - Presentation of Data in JSON format:

```json
[
  {
    "name": "Arithmetic Mean (AM)",
    "formula": "\\bar{x} = \\frac{\\sum x_i}{n}",
    "variables": "x_i = individual data points, n = number of observations",
    "units": "Depends on the data",
    "notes": "Used to calculate the average value of a dataset"
  },
  {
    "name": "Geometric Mean (GM)",
    "formula": "\\bar{x} = \\sqrt[n]{\\prod x_i}",
    "variables": "x_i = individual data points, n = number of observations",
    "units": "Depends on the data",
    "notes": "Used to calculate the average value of a dataset when data is in the form of growth rates or percentages"
  },
  {
    "name": "Harmonic Mean (HM)",
    "formula": "HM = \\frac{n}{\\sum \\frac{1}{x_i}}",
    "variables": "x_i = individual data points, n = number of observations",
    "units": "Depends on the data",
    "notes": "Used to calculate the average value of a dataset when data represents rates or ratios"
  },
  {
    "name": "Coefficient of Range (CR)",
    "formula": "CR = \\frac{R}{x_{max} - x_{min}}",
    "variables": "R = range, x_{max} = maximum value, x_{min} = minimum value",
    "units": "Depends on the data",
    "notes": "Used to measure the dispersion of a dataset"
  },
  {
    "name": "Coefficient of Quartile Deviation (CQD)",
    "formula": "CQD = \\frac{Q_3 - Q_1}{2}",
    "variables": "Q_3 = third quartile, Q_1 = first quartile",
    "units": "Depends on the data",
    "notes": "Used to measure the dispersion of a dataset"
  },
  {
    "name": "Coefficient of Variation (CV)",
    "formula": "CV = \\left(\\frac{\\sigma}{\\bar{x}}\\right) \\times 100",
    "variables": "\\sigma = standard deviation, \\bar{x} = mean",
    "units": "Percentage",
    "notes": "Used to measure the relative dispersion of a dataset"
  },
  {
    "name": "Standard Deviation (σ)",
    "formula": "\\sigma = \\sqrt{\\frac{\\sum (x_i - \\bar{x})^2}{n}}",
    "variables": "x_i = individual data points, \\bar{x} = mean, n = number of observations",
    "units": "Depends on the data",
    "notes": "Used to measure the dispersion of a dataset"
  },
  {
    "name": "Variance (σ²)",
    "formula": "\\sigma^2 = \\frac{\\sum (x_i - \\bar{x})^2}{n}",
    "variables": "x_i = individual data points, \\bar{x} = mean, n = number of observations",
    "units": "Depends on the data",
    "notes": "Used to measure the dispersion of a dataset"
  },
  {
    "name": "Range Rule of Thumb",
    "formula": "The range should be between 1/3 and 2/3 of the mean",
    "variables": "Range, mean",
    "units": "Depends on the data",
    "notes": "Used to check for outliers in a dataset"
  },
  {
    "name": "Empirical Relationship between Mean Deviation and Standard Deviation",
    "formula": "\\bar{d} = k\\sigma",
    "variables": "\\bar{d} = mean deviation, k = constant, \\sigma = standard deviation",
    "units": "Depends on the data",
    "notes": "Used to find the mean deviation from the mean"
  },
  {
    "name": "Chebyshev's Theorem",
    "formula": "P(|x - \\mu| \\geq k\\sigma) \\leq \\frac{1}{k^2}",
    "variables": "P = probability, \\mu = mean, \\sigma = standard deviation, k = constant",
    "units": "Depends on the data",
    "notes": "Used to estimate the probability of an event occurring"
  },
  {
    "name": "Empirical Relationship between Range and Standard Deviation",
    "formula": "R = k\\sigma",
    "variables": "R = range, k = constant, \\sigma = standard deviation",
    "units": "Depends on the data",
    "notes": "Used to find the range of a dataset"
  },
  {
    "name": "Five-Number Summary",
    "formula": "[\\min, Q_1, \\bar{x}, Q_3, \\max]",
    "variables": "\\min = minimum value, Q_1 = first quartile, \\bar{x} = mean, Q_3 = third quartile, \\max = maximum value",
    "units": "Depends on the data",
    "notes": "Used to summarize a dataset in a concise manner"
  }
]
```

Note: This list is not exhaustive but covers most of the important formulas and laws related to the presentation of data in CBSE Class 11 Economics.
